overview.Summary: The FLORAHOX Activity campaign initiates with a phishing attack targeting employees in the communications sector, followed by credential extraction and ultimately leading to service disruption.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Stealing sensitive communications data and disrupting services.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 164 Name: Mobile Phishing Description: Targets mobile users with phishing for sensitive info.
stage.description: Adversaries send phishing emails to communications sector employees, leveraging CVE-2024-57727 to gain initial access through a Web-based attack on internet-facing assets.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 226 Name: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation Description: Manipulates session credentials for unauthorized access.
stage.description: After initial access, adversaries execute credential-dumping activities using CVE-2015-4068, targeting application-level credentials to facilitate further network traversal.

stage.name: Impact
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 607 Name: Obstruction Description: Interrupts system interactions to degrade functionality.
stage.description: Adversaries disrupt communication services by obstructing critical system interactions, shutting down operations to achieve their disruptive objective.

Scenario.summary: The FLORAHOX Activity campaign begins with a phishing attack targeting communications sector employees, followed by credential extraction and culminates in service disruption through obstruction techniques.
